title: Webhooks, Replay Defense, SSRF, Abuse, and Sensitive Business Flows · Mastery Check
summary: A production-readiness review compares near-miss decisions for webhooks, replay defense, ssrf, abuse, and sensitive business flows and requires the smallest safe webhook authenticity, replay, and abuse defense with changed-request evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for webhook authenticity, replay, and abuse defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound and retain exact raw body bytes, select expected signature scheme and key, compare in constant time, and only then parse and authorize the event.
Validate timestamp or nonce window, event ID uniqueness, tenant, type, ordering, duplicate delivery, and durable processing state before effects.
Acknowledge only after durable acceptance, move long work to bounded processing, classify retries, make effects idempotent, and expose operator recovery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Business Flow Abuse
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz team handles case 1119 in a recovery rehearsal with an ambiguous database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine raw-message webhook authentication, replay bounds, and SSRF egress policy; then cancel during the dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound costly domain actions by principal, resource, workflow state, velocity, inventory, and anomaly evidence beyond raw request rate.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
